sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, I can see:
1. The charcoal shape is now on the far left (cols ~10-18), fully visible as an L-shape
2. The black shape with white dots is on the right, overlapping with the yellow shape
3. The yellow shape is partially covered by the black shape
This is very interesting! The black shape has moved to overlap with the yellow shape. Let me check the segmentation to see what happened to the yellow shape and the white dots.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current segmentation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5 and n['color'] not in ('b', 'S'):
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"  WHITE: id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        print(f"  CHARCOAL: id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Charcoal shape: 45 pixels (back to full size!), at cols 9-17 (far left)
- Yellow shape: 27 pixels (was 45, now shrunk!), at rows 45-47, cols 51-59 (just the top bar)
- Black shape: 40 pixels, at cols 45-53 (overlapping with yellow shape)
- White dots: 3 dots visible (id=8 at row 49, col 46; id=9 at row 49, col 49; id=12 at row 55, col 52)
So the black shape overlapped with the yellow shape, and the yellow shape shrank from 45 to 27 pixels! This is the same mechanic as with the charcoal shape - when the black shape overlaps with another shape, that shape shrinks.
The yellow shape was an L-shape (45 pixels) and is now just the top horizontal bar (27 pixels). The vertical stem was consumed by the black shape.
So the mechanic is clear now:
- The black shape "consumes" other shapes when they overlap
- The charcoal shape and yellow shape both shrink when the black shape overlaps them
- The goal might be to completely consume the yellow shape (or charcoal shape)
But wait, the charcoal shape is now 45 pixels (full size) because it's far from the black shape. And the yellow shape is 27 pixels because it's partially overlapped by the black shape.
So the goal might be to:
1. Move the black shape to completely overlap with the yellow shape
2. This would consume the yellow shape completely (0 pixels)
3. This completes the level
Let me try pressing RIGHT more to move the black shape further into the yellow shape.
Actually, looking at the positions:
- Black shape: cols 45-53
- Yellow shape: cols 51-59 (but now only rows 45-47, the top bar)
The black shape is overlapping with the left part of the yellow shape's top bar. To consume the rest of the yellow shape, I need to move the black shape further right.
But wait, the yellow shape is now just the top bar (rows 45-47). The vertical stem (rows 48-53) was consumed. So the black shape already consumed part of the yellow shape.
Let me try pressing RIGHT more to see if the yellow shape shrinks further.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Yellow shape shrunk from 45 to 27 pixels when black shape overlapped
# Try pressing RIGHT more to consume the rest of the yellow shape
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
